title: Calcearia naviculisepala
intro: Calceolaria naviculisepala, commonly known as the Boat Lip Calceolaria, is a flowering plant species in the Calceolaria genus, native to the Andean regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive disorders, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Calceolaria naviculisepala include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Calceolaria naviculisepala is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and to reduce inflammation and promote digestion.
